description: Lock consumerism to a postwar market-consumption order driven by mass advertising, mass-produced consumer goods, and capitalist business incentives.

checks.reason: The node remains anchored to mid-twentieth-century postwar mass consumption rather than older conspicuous-consumption concepts or later digital consumer targeting.
Advertising is the direct mass-media demand-creation mechanism in the scoped consumerism claim.
Mass production scales abundant standardized consumer goods, but it is not a single required method for every consumer practice.
Industrial capitalism supplies the market, business, and profit context for consumer-spending growth.
Generic mass media is broader than the advertising-driven demand mechanism in the node scope.
Psychology is contextual consumer-behavior analysis, not a direct prerequisite for the postwar consumption order.
